item.title: FirstFT: Russia sending drones to Iran, says western intelligence
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Ferrari flies supercars to Gulf and SpaceX boosts IPO ambitions

item.title: FT 1000: Europe’s Fastest-Growing Companies
item.description: The 2026 ranking highlights the businesses that are flourishing in uncertain times. From financial services driving growth in London to a personalised dog food brand in Italy. Plus the role of venture capital and France’s tech hub success

item.title: Paul Weiss, its star client and Epstein
item.description: The DoJ’s Epstein files reveal just how far law firm chair Brad Karp was willing to go for his most important client

item.title: Battersea Power Station held talks over sale of land at centre of dispute
item.description: Discussions took place before the FT reported that the site’s former CEO had raised concerns about valuations

item.title: Ferrari flies personalised supercars to super-rich Middle East buyers
item.description: Premium manufacturers keep delivering to lucrative customers despite sea freight disruption

item.title: EY sets aside record £188mn for fines and legal claims
item.description: Filings show Big Four firm has added to pot of cash as it battles multiple probes linked to quality of its audit work

item.title: Banks face a new triple threat: Iran, AI and private credit
item.description: Bigger issues will keep casting a shadow over European lender’s stocks

item.title: Iraq’s economy teeters as oil sales collapse due to Iran war
item.description: Crude exports have fallen by more than 70% leading to a major shortfall in the state budget

item.title: Bidders circle as EDF’s renewable assets in the US go on sale
item.description: KKR, Clearway Energy and LS Power among groups interested in acquiring French group’s American clean energy business

item.title: How to survive an energy crunch
item.description: Have you considered simply doing less?

item.title: France leads the pack in the fastest-growing companies ranking
item.description: Sustained government initiatives over the past 10 years have helped the country establish itself as a key tech hub

item.title: Financial services helps London retain title of top hub
item.description: Banks, fintechs and insurers choose the capital for its rich mix of professional services and regulators

item.title: Apollo, Paul Weiss and Epstein: how a star client brought down a Big Law boss
item.description: Apollo’s Leon Black asked lawyer Brad Karp to settle a fee dispute with Jeffrey Epstein. It was the start of a years-long relationship

item.title: FT1000: Europe’s Fastest-Growing Companies 2026 ranking
item.description: Many businesses are thriving despite lacklustre economies and geopolitical volatility

item.title: Italy’s innovative start-ups work hard to secure capital
item.description: Crowdfunding platforms and venture capital funds are helping the country’s young companies expand

item.title: Venture capital: a crucial backer of fast-growing businesses
item.description: More than a third of the €66.2bn worth of VC deals in Europe were in AI-related companies

item.title: IT and software groups dominate the FT1000 ranking
item.description: From digital image software to payment services, these are the companies that delivered strong growth

item.title: Investors try to stay ahead of Trump’s Iran moves
item.description: US president appears to make abrupt policy pivots based on swings in crude prices

item.title: Self-driving will not make Nissan a commodity, says CEO
item.description: Ivan Espinosa bets the future of the beleaguered Japanese carmaker on its autonomous technology

item.title: Hong Kong weighs ‘big bang’ tax cuts for asset managers
item.description: Expansion of carried interest regime could mean zero levies on performance fees at hedge funds

item.title: The rise of China’s hottest new commodity: AI tokens
item.description: Chinese AI models made by groups such as DeepSeek and MiniMax have overtaken US rivals in token consumption

item.title: Harry Potter TV reboot will be ‘biggest thing’ HBO has ever done
item.description: Warner Bros Discovery wants to grow its HBO Max streaming service in UK ahead of Paramount takeover

item.title: SpaceX boosts IPO ambition with plans to raise $75bn
item.description: Elon Musk’s rocket company told investors it hopes to go public with a $1.75tn valuation

item.title: Meta and Google liable for harm to children’s mental health in landmark US case
item.description: Jury awards at least $3mn in damages with Instagram owner to pay the majority

item.title: Republicans back Iran war even as voters feel ‘Biden-level’ petrol price pain
item.description: Lawmakers aim to reassure constituents that cost increases are temporary

item.title: Monte dei Paschi revokes chief executive’s powers
item.description: Lender’s move against Luigi Lovaglio comes after he challenged a plan to replace him

item.title: Russia sending drones to Iran, western intelligence says
item.description: Moscow close to completing phased deliveries of lethal weapons, food and medicine to Tehran

item.title: Jefferies reports new $10mn First Brands loss as it writes off exposure
item.description: Investment fund owned by bank provided off-balance sheet financing to collapsed car parts supplier

item.title: The other Strait of Hormuz shock
item.description: Disruption to commodities far beyond oil and gas will have a long-lasting impact

item.title: Elliott offers Synopsys a masterclass in throwing its weight around
item.description: This chip design software maker could have and will have much bigger profits if the activist investor has anything to do with it
